summary: This cluster focuses on the discovery and taxonomic characterization of the epiphytic dinoflagellate *Amphidinium massartii* in a new temperate geographic region. Researchers isolated the species from the waters off Jeju Island, Korea, and identified it through a combination of morphological analysis and rDNA sequencing, including LSU, small subunit, and cytochrome b markers. The Korean strains were found to possess unique rDNA sequences that distinguished them from previously known Australian and USA strains, confirming a distinct lineage. This work expands the known biogeographic range of *A. massartii* and underscores the value of molecular phylogenetics in resolving cryptic diversity within epiphytic dinoflagellates.
